trivia_bullets.text: Etched Cornfield is part of Duskmourn's ten-card "unlucky lands" cycle of common dual lands, all of which enter tapped unless a player has 13 or less life. The number 13 is a deliberate triskaidekaphobia motif running through the set. The cycle was previewed September 2, 2024 by CoolStuffInc as an exclusive. [CoolStuffInc: "Ten New Common Dual Lands in Duskmourn: House of Horrors," Paige Smith, 2024-09-02; Unlucky land — MTG Wiki, via WebSearch snippet]
sources: CoolStuffInc: Ten New Common Dual Lands in Duskmourn, Paige Smith (2024-09-02)
Unlucky land — MTG Wiki, via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Artist Randy Gallegos illustrated multiple land cards for Duskmourn, including Etched Cornfield and Floodfarm Verge. In a post on his personal blog (gallegosart.com, August 30, 2024) discussing his Duskmourn work, Gallegos noted that Floodfarm Verge was his favorite of the three pieces he contributed. Gallegos has a long MTG illustration career spanning sets including Lord of the Rings: Tales of Middle-earth, Doctor Who, Ravnica Allegiance, Modern Horizons 3, and The Lost Caverns of Ixalan. [Randy Gallegos, gallegosart.com blog post, 2024-08-30; WebSearch result]

Flavor text: "They say those who lose their way in the fields gradually wither and grow brittle, until at last they put down roots and anchor into the soil as a whispering stalk." The flavor describes transformation-into-plant as punishment for getting lost, consistent with the plane's predatory-terrain design: the House's cornfields absorb wanderers. [Scryfall]

Etched Cornfield produces green or white mana. Its EDHREC rank of 10669 is modestly lower than Bleeding Woods (9521), reflecting that the G/W color pairing while useful is less universally splashable than R/G in Commander mana bases. Legal in Pauper, making it one of the few dual-mana sources at common available in that format. [Scryfall legalities and EDHREC rank via Scryfall API]
